mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators looking to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This repository contains the necessary charts for installation.

steps.title: Install the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are required for Cluster API to function correctly, as they define the custom resources used by the operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs for Cluster API.

steps.title: Install Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API controller components into the specified namespace.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

```

> This command deploys the Cluster API components.

steps.title: Create a Cluster API custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster API custom resource to manage your Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infra-cluster
EOF

```

> Replace 'MyInfrastructureProviderCluster' with your actual infrastructure provider.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infra-cluster
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Cluster API components to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> You should see the Cluster API controller pods running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this will delete all managed clusters and associated resources, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> This command removes the Cluster API Helm release.
> This command deletes the CRDs associated with Cluster API.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your clusters. This process includes draining nodes if necessary, upgrading the components, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Drains all nodes in the cluster.
> This command upgrades the Cluster API components.
> Verify that the upgraded pods are running.
commands: kubectl get clusters -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD has not been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRD installation command.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Cluster API controller is not responding or is overloaded.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Cluster API controller pods for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in kube-system are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster API components are failing to start, possibly due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Inspect the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: infrastructure provider not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified infrastructure provider is not installed or configured.

**Fix:**
Ensure the correct infrastructure provider is installed and referenced in your Cluster custom resource.

versionNotes.changes: Fixed issues with cluster scaling and improved error messages for better debugging.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system' showing all Cluster API controller pods in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets:

metadata:

  namespace: default
spec:

    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infra-cluster
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
